sections.heading: Radon Testing in Northampton, MA
sections.meta: Standalone: $150 · As add-on: $125 with home inspection · Results within 48 hours of monitor pickup

sections.heading: What Northampton Buyers and Owners Need to Know

blocks.html: Northampton sits in a moderate radon zone — generally lower than Pelham-side Amherst, but Florence and Leeds bedrock wells and basements still produce elevated readings, and downtown fieldstone basements leak soil gas through mortar joints. We run 48-hour continuous monitors under closed-house conditions, with attention to finished basements in Victorians where bedrooms or offices push exposure time up significantly.

Ken Arnold has been performing radon testing services across the Pioneer Valley since 2004 — including hundreds of Northampton-area properties. Every inspection is performed personally by Ken (MA License #587), follows ASHI standards, and is delivered as a comprehensive photo-documented digital report.
48-hour continuous-monitor radon testing in the Pioneer Valley. Add to any home inspection or schedule standalone.
For full scope, methodology, and pricing details, see the canonical service page: Radon Testing. For broader context on Northampton's housing stock, see the Northampton town profile.

items.q: What radon level is concerning in Massachusetts?
items.a: The EPA action level is 4.0 pCi/L. The Massachusetts Department of Public Health recommends mitigation at 4.0 pCi/L or higher, and considering it between 2.0 and 4.0. Many Pioneer Valley homes test in the 4–10 range; test results above 10 pCi/L are not unusual in Greenfield, Holyoke, and the Mount Toby area of Sunderland.

We place a continuous-monitoring device on the lowest livable level of the home — typically the basement or first floor — for at least 48 hours under closed-house conditions (windows closed except for normal entry/exit). The monitor records hourly readings and provides a final average plus a graph of how levels varied over time.

items.q: What if my test comes back high?
items.a: Mitigation is straightforward. Most Pioneer Valley homes can be mitigated for $1,200–$2,500 with a sub-slab depressurization system installed by a licensed mitigator. Post-mitigation re-testing typically brings levels well below 2.0 pCi/L. We can refer you to mitigation contractors if needed; we don't take referral fees.

items.q: Can I test the home myself?
items.a: Short-term DIY canisters work for general awareness but they're not generally accepted in real-estate transactions. For a purchase, use a continuous-monitoring test performed by a licensed inspector — that's what almost every Massachusetts buyer's representative will accept.

items.q: Should I test a new construction home?
items.a: Absolutely. New construction in the Pioneer Valley regularly tests above the EPA action level despite tighter envelopes — modern homes are simply more airtight, which means lower air-exchange rates and higher indoor radon concentration. Mitigation is dramatically cheaper if installed during construction; testing before closing is critical.

cards.title: Buyer Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: A thorough, independent inspection of every major system before you close — 22 years of Pioneer Valley experience documented in a detailed photo report.

cards.title: Pre-Listing Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Find out what a buyer's inspector will find — before you list. Get ahead of objections, price the home accurately, and shorten your time on market.

cards.title: New Construction Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: An independent inspection before your warranty closes. Phase, pre-drywall, or final-walk inspections — finding what the builder's punch list misses.

cards.title: Condo & Townhouse Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Targeted inspection of your unit plus the shared-system review most condo buyers don't get — common to mill conversions, downtown buildings, and townhouse complexes across Western Mass.

cards.title: Well Water Testing in Northampton
cards.body: Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.

cards.title: Septic System Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Pre-purchase septic system evaluation for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Coordinated with Massachusetts Title 5 inspectors when transfer requires it.

cards.title: Termite & WDI Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.

cards.title: Mold Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Visual mold evaluation with moisture-source identification, optional air sampling. Common in older Pioneer Valley basements and mill-conversion units.
